annotations.label: Rectangle

anchor.snippet: a rectangle is a rectilinear convex polygon or a quadrilateral with four right angles

annotations.note: Mathlib only defines an axis-parallel rectangle as a set in ℂ, not a general quadrilateral with four right angles.

annotations.label: Square (special rectangle)

anchor.snippet: A rectangle with four sides of equal length is a square

annotations.note: No geometric Square quadrilateral definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Oblong

anchor.snippet: The term " oblong " is used to refer to a non- square rectangle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of oblong rectangles.

annotations.label: Crossed rectangle

anchor.snippet: A crossed rectangle is a crossed (self-intersecting) quadrilateral which consists of two opposite sides of a rectangle along with the two diagonals

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of crossed/self-intersecting quadrilaterals.

annotations.label: Characterizations of a rectangle

anchor.snippet: A convex quadrilateral is a rectangle if and only if it is any one of the following

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement characterizes a quadrilateral as a rectangle.

annotations.label: Rectangle as special parallelogram

anchor.snippet: A rectangle is a special case of a parallelogram in which each pair of adjacent sides is perpendicular

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Parallelogram type relating geometric rectangles to parallelograms.

annotations.label: Parallelogram as special trapezium

anchor.snippet: A parallelogram is a special case of a trapezium

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Trapezium type, so this hierarchy is not formalized.

annotations.label: Trapezium

anchor.snippet: A trapezium is a convex quadrilateral which has at least one pair of parallel opposite sides

annotations.note: Mathlib has only the trapezoidal-rule integral approximation, not the geometric trapezium.

annotations.label: Simple convex quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: The boundary does not cross itself

annotations.note: No formal notion of simple convex quadrilateral exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Star-shaped convex quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: The whole interior is visible from a single point, without crossing any edge

annotations.note: Mathlib has StarConvex sets but no star-shaped quadrilateral type.

annotations.label: De Villiers' rectangle

anchor.snippet: De Villiers defines a rectangle more generally as any quadrilateral with axes of symmetry through each pair of opposite sides

annotations.note: No De Villiers-style generalized rectangle is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rectangle is cyclic

anchor.snippet: A rectangle is cyclic : all corners lie on a single circle

annotations.note: No theorem stating geometric rectangles are cyclic is found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rectangle is equiangular

anchor.snippet: It is equiangular : all its corner angles are equal

annotations.note: No equiangular-polygon theory for rectangles exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rectangle is isogonal

anchor.snippet: It is isogonal or vertex-transitive : all corners lie within the same symmetry orbit

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize vertex-transitivity of geometric rectangles.

annotations.label: Reflectional and rotational symmetry

anchor.snippet: It has two lines of reflectional symmetry and rotational symmetry of order 2

annotations.note: No symmetry-group statement about rectangles is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual of a rectangle is a rhombus

anchor.snippet: The dual polygon of a rectangle is a rhombus

annotations.note: Mathlib has no polygon-duality framework or rhombus type.

annotations.label: Varignon-type rhombus from rectangle midpoints

anchor.snippet: The figure formed by joining, in order, the midpoints of the sides of a rectangle is a rhombus and vice versa

annotations.note: Varignon's theorem and rhombus characterization are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rectangle is rectilinear polygon

anchor.snippet: A rectangle is a rectilinear polygon : its sides meet at right angles

annotations.note: No rectilinear-polygon theory is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Five degrees of freedom

anchor.snippet: A rectangle in the plane can be defined by five independent degrees of freedom

annotations.note: Degree-of-freedom count for planar rectangles is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incomparable rectangles

anchor.snippet: Two rectangles, neither of which will fit inside the other, are said to be incomparable

annotations.note: No incomparable-rectangles definition is found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area, perimeter, diagonal formulae

anchor.snippet: If a rectangle has length

annotations.note: Mathlib has measure-theoretic area of the complex Rectangle set but no symbolic area = ab / perimeter = 2(a+b) / diagonal formula for a geometric rectangle.

annotations.label: Isoperimetric theorem for rectangles

anchor.snippet: The isoperimetric theorem for rectangles states that among all rectangles of a given perimeter , the square has the largest area

annotations.note: No isoperimetric inequality for rectangles appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Midpoints of perpendicular-diagonal quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: The midpoints of the sides of any quadrilateral with perpendicular diagonals form a rectangle

annotations.note: This midpoint-rectangle theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parallelogram with equal diagonals

anchor.snippet: A parallelogram with equal diagonals is a rectangle

annotations.note: No theorem characterizing rectangles among parallelograms by equal diagonals exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Japanese theorem for cyclic quadrilaterals

anchor.snippet: The Japanese theorem for cyclic quadrilaterals

annotations.note: Mathlib's only 'Japanese' results concern Japanese brackets, not the Japanese theorem.

annotations.label: British flag theorem

anchor.snippet: The British flag theorem states that with vertices denoted

annotations.note: The British flag theorem is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inscribed/circumscribed homothetic rectangle

anchor.snippet: For every convex body C in the plane, we can inscribe a rectangle r in C

annotations.note: No inscribed/homothetic-rectangle theorem for convex bodies is found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unique folding rectangle

anchor.snippet: There exists a unique rectangle with sides

annotations.note: No folding/unique-rectangle existence statement appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Crossed quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A crossed quadrilateral (self-intersecting) consists of two opposite sides of a non-self-intersecting quadrilateral along with the two diagonals

annotations.note: Mathlib has no crossed-quadrilateral concept.

annotations.label: Crossed rectangle (detailed)

anchor.snippet: a crossed rectangle is a crossed quadrilateral which consists of two opposite sides of a rectangle along with the two diagonals

annotations.note: Mathlib has no crossed-rectangle definition.

annotations.label: Polygon density of crossed rectangle

anchor.snippet: The interior of a crossed rectangle can have a polygon density of

annotations.note: Polygon density is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Crossed rectangle interior angle sum

anchor.snippet: the sum of its interior angles is 720°

annotations.note: No interior-angle-sum statement for crossed quadrilaterals is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shared properties of rectangle and crossed rectangle

anchor.snippet: A rectangle and a crossed rectangle are quadrilaterals with the following properties in common

annotations.note: Mathlib has no comparison of crossed-rectangle and rectangle properties.

annotations.label: Spherical rectangle

anchor.snippet: a spherical rectangle is a figure whose four edges are great circle arcs which meet at equal angles greater than 90°

annotations.note: No spherical-rectangle concept appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic rectangle

anchor.snippet: an elliptic rectangle is a figure in the elliptic plane whose four edges are elliptic arcs which meet at equal angles greater than 90°

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize elliptic-plane rectangles.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic rectangle

anchor.snippet: a hyperbolic rectangle is a figure in the hyperbolic plane whose four edges are hyperbolic arcs which meet at equal angles less than 90°

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize hyperbolic-plane rectangles.

annotations.label: Squared, rectangled, triangulated rectangle

anchor.section: Squared, perfect, and other tiled rectangles
anchor.snippet: A rectangle tiled by squares, rectangles, or triangles is said to be a "squared", "rectangled", or "triangulated"

annotations.note: No tiling-of-rectangles taxonomy exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Perfect tiled rectangle

anchor.section: Squared, perfect, and other tiled rectangles
anchor.snippet: The tiled rectangle is perfect

annotations.note: Perfect tilings of rectangles are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Commensurable sides characterization

anchor.section: Squared, perfect, and other tiled rectangles
anchor.snippet: A rectangle has commensurable sides if and only if it is tileable by a finite number of unequal squares

annotations.note: Dehn's commensurability/squared-rectangle theorem is not in Mathlib.
